author: @RICOFRITO
text: AI is the equivalent of “good enough.” Real human-made art exists because someone wanted something expressed exactly the way they envisioned it.

replies.author: @2001SpaceCowboy_
replies.text: So it’s shit that you settle for? This isn’t a good thing something being “good enough”

replies.author: @RICOFRITO
replies.text:
@2001SpaceCowboy_ I never said I settle for it. I’m saying that AI is widely being used because it’s “good enough” for people who just want something fast or serviceable.
I’m an artist, and I don’t like AI slop. But Pandora’s box is already open, and creatives have to deal with that reality. I’ll always choose human-made art over AI output, because intention and authorship actually matter.

replies.author: @2001SpaceCowboy_
replies.text: @RICOFRITOI misunderstood and apologise x

replies.author: @RICOFRITO
replies.text: @2001SpaceCowboy_ No problem, you have a great day!

author: @lexicon1742
text: The Beggar's Canyon video works better than most of the AI videos (slop or otherwise) because the guy that's making it actually knows how to frame and shoot a real film. They're actually using it as a tool to create something they couldn't with the budget, manpower and equipment they have access to. It's like how for Jurassic Park they still employed the claymation director to help bring the dinosaurs' movement to life when they switched to CGI from stop-motion. The expertise very much still matters.

author: @zombiejesus7445
text:
I have thought about "AI" for a long time now.. I have come to 2 conclusions

1. People need to stop using it as a buzzy word like they did with NFTs... Learn what it really is like you have. 
1a. Banks need to stop saying "we use AI"... Then still don't even detect when there is a purchase on a debate card that was scanned outside of the country... AI would actually see that the transaction is out of the norm and raise a flag to it
2. Computer learning has been a thing since 1950s. AI is completely different and we are still not out of computer learning. 
2a. Computer learning is x + y = z. AI is compiling a set of various data and coming up with multiple conclusions and sorting them by probability and picking the top, like what the human brain does. 

I love your videos. I am glad you didn't fall to the darkside

author: @Joel.Derrick
text: AI results are dependent on the creator - not the tool.   Everything depends about "The Tool in the Hands of the Creator" - when "TRUE" filmmakers have command of a tool, the results are entirely different then the laymen.  You can give a 100K camera to a 2 year film student and an iPhone to a DP for 20 years who is adept and the DP will create a superior product every time.  Individuals are not creatively challenged to make the best content - if you're already dealing with an 50-year old IP / created world and characters and crafted  personalities already established .  To make that great you better have a great storyteller that knows how to WIELD the tools but have an eye that allows a story to stand alone DESPITE the tool - not an opinion - FACT!

author: @StayFractalesque
text: honestly, after watching all those star wars AI videos.. like, yeah, theyre cool.. but the Editing is S__T ..what did they use AI to edit it too?? ..zero pacing, baffling shot compositions.. ..its like theyre obsessed with Every Second the AI produced.. instead of editing around AI shortcomings, they Showcased the AI Shortcomings.. ..

replies.author: @Doom-Shepard
replies.text: That's kind of the problem with AI - you can't edit around those, when the shortcomings are all you have. You get 5-10 second long clips that have no logical consistency, and are filled with hallucinations. If you tried to edit that down, then you'd get, what, 1-2 second long scenes? No one would be able to watch that. The only way to keep the AI consistent is to run it over pre-existing footage, like for example what the guy who makes the Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast "80s live action" AI videos, he runs the in-game cutscenes through AI, and it's way more consistent than AI by itself is (although it still suffers from things like character outfits randomly changing in look and material, and haircuts not staying in place etc.)

replies.author: @StayFractalesque
replies.text: ​@Doom-Shepard yes all that is true, but i still think it could have been saved in the edit, especially the fight scenes..

replies.author: @Doc_Cocked_Glock

author: @Imylover
text: Perfect summery, Theory! And I love that you use your own experiences as a film maker to give perfect examples. Many point out the AI problems with the lip movements, or the stiff voices or that you can't get a proper choreography of any sort of fighting done right yet. And that's all true. But I have taken notice of a very returning AI problem that next to no one bring up, but you did. The sounds! Often when a character, any character, in an AI fan film is just walking the sound is totally off, usually far too loud to sound realistic to the person you are watching & the ground said person is walking on. And often the pace of the legs & the sound of the feet touching the floor are also not in sync. But one of my biggest problems with the SW AI fan films are the scores. The music. I guess & realize that they can't use the real SW music, copyrights & all that. But the music these creators do pick is more than 85% all wrong. You even hear uplifting music when you are meant to feel sad! And the score in SW is like the Force is to a Jedi / Sith. It has to feel right or it won't work. Now, sound effects & music can absolutely over time be handled better & be fixed. Just as you say, more knowledge & experience is needed. I am just a bit sad that the sound & music in the SW AI fan films never seem to get any form of priority from the creators. The few that do get it right, that is when I honestly DO feel real SW coming back, regardless of what it looks like.

author: @DarkPassengrr
text: The reality is it is a tool, and if you don’t eventually adapt and incorporate it you will fall behind. Trying to ignore it and hope it goes away is what the SFX artists did when CGI came out. Then CGI became standard. Same with AI, it’s in its early years much like CGI was in the 90’s but like you said, it will continue to progress. You can now directly control the camera for example as of this year. I don’t think you should put all your eggs into one basket but I think finding a place for it in your workflow is going to also become the standard as CGI is now. Might as well embrace it sooner than later and use it to your advantage because if you don’t, others will, and why handicap yourself in an already highly oversaturated environment. Just my thoughts! Can’t wait for Vader Episode 2, whatever “tools” you choose to use I will enjoy it. May the force be with you!
